summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that Spree Commerce includes user_id in the permitted checkout attributes, enabling mass assignment of the order's user_id to any arbitrary user through the storefront API without authentication or user_id ownership validation.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should confirm that :user_id is in @@checkout_attributes, that the checkout update flow permits it through mass assignment, and that no user_id ownership validation exists. The proof signal EVOHUNT_PROOF_mass_assignment_user_id_confirmed should be emitted.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Docker image, cloned the target repository, mounted it into the container, and executed the exploit script. The script read the Spree source code from /target-repo, extracted @@checkout_attributes from permitted_attributes.rb, confirmed :user_id is in the list, verified the checkout update flow permits it, confirmed no user_id ownership validation exists in checkout/update.rb, and demonstrated that a simulated permit() call would pass user_id through to order.update(). The proof signal was emitted successfully.
verification_method: The exploit script reads the actual Spree source files from /target-repo and performs the following checks: (1) Extracts @@checkout_attributes from permitted_attributes.rb and verifies :user_id is in the list. (2) Reads strong_parameters.rb and confirms permitted_checkout_attributes includes checkout_attributes. (3) Reads checkout_controller.rb and confirms permitted_checkout_attributes is passed to the update service. (4) Reads order/checkout.rb and confirms update_from_params calls permit(permitted_params). (5) Reads checkout/update.rb and confirms no user_id ownership validation exists. (6) Simulates the mass assignment by running Ruby's permit logic against the extracted attribute list with an attacker-controlled payload {user_id: 999}. The script exits with code 0 only when ALL checks pass and emits the proof signal EVOHUNT_PROOF_mass_assignment_user_id_confirmed.
evidence_produced_by_run: exploit.rb confirmed :user_id is present in the checkout attributes list
exploit.rb confirmed strong_parameters.rb:19-26 propagates checkout_attributes into permitted_checkout_attributes
exploit.rb confirmed checkout_controller.rb:40 passes permitted_checkout_attributes to the update service
exploit.rb confirmed order/checkout.rb:233 calls permit(permitted_params) which includes user_id
exploit.rb confirmed checkout/update.rb has no user_id ownership validation (only address ownership is validated)
exploit.rb confirmed create_payment and select_shipping_method actions lack spree_authorize! calls
exploit.rb simulated permit() with attacker payload {user_id: 999} and confirmed user_id passes through to the update call

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the v4.10.2 tag, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
